citations.narrative:
In November 2024, the facility moved their USDA-regulated animals to a new facility. They did not update Animal Care
with their new site address.
Failure to update Animal Care with a new site address prevents the inspector from locating the animals and performing
routine, unannounced inspections.
The facility has since updated Animal Care with their new address. The licensee shall ensure from this day forward
(March 10, 2026) to notify Animal Care no fewer than 90 days before any change in the name, address, substantial
control or ownership of his business or operation, locations, activities, and number or type of animals.

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The facility does not have a complete program of veterinary care.
The Program of Veterinary Care provided during the inspection did not list any animals. The words "Virginia opossum"
were written, then crossed out and replaced with "N/A". A representative hypothesized that this was referring to the
facility's previous opossum which had passed away. The facility currently has a new Virginia opossum and a groundhog.
In addition, the facility has three preventative medicines listed for parasite prevention and control. The facility is not
currently administering those medicines to either of their residents. A representative was unsure which species of animal
the written preventative medicines was intended for (as they previously also housed a coyote), but hypothesized that it
was for also their previous Virginia opossum.
A written, current program of veterinary care specific to the facility and resident animal species is necessary to facilitate
communication between the attending veterinarian, the on-site animal care staff and the leadership team. It ensures
animals will receive consistent and timely veterinary diagnoses, prevention, and treatment of diseases, illnesses, and
injuries.
Each exhibitor with a part time attending veterinarian under formal arrangements shall establish and maintain a written
program of veterinary care.
Correction date: March 24, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.1(b)(1) 
   
Requirements and application 
In November 2024, the facility moved their USDA-regulated animals to a new facility. They did not update Animal Care 
with their new site address. 
 
Failure to update Animal Care with a new site address prevents the inspector from locating the animals and performing 
routine, unannounced inspections.  
 
The facility has since updated Animal Care with their new address. The licensee shall ensure from this day forward 
(March 10, 2026) to notify Animal Care no fewer than 90 days before any change in the name, address, substantial 
control or ownership of his business or operation, locations, activities, and number or type of animals. 
2.40(a)(1) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The facility does not have a complete program of veterinary care.  
 
The Program of Veterinary Care provided during the inspection did not list any animals. The words "Virginia opossum" 
were written, then crossed out and replaced with "N/A". A representative hypothesized that this was referring to the 
facility's previous opossum which had passed away. The facility currently has a new Virginia opossum and a groundhog.  
 
In addition, the facility has three preventative medicines listed for parasite prevention and control. The facility is not 
currently administering those medicines to either of their residents. A representative was unsure which species of animal 
the written preventative medicines was intended for (as they previously also housed a coyote), but hypothesized that it 
was for also their previous Virginia opossum.  
 
A written, current program of veterinary care specific to the facility and resident animal species is necessary to facilitate 
communication between the attending veterinarian, the on-site animal care staff and the leadership team. It ensures 
animals will receive consistent and timely veterinary diagnoses, prevention, and treatment of diseases, illnesses, and 
injuries.

Each exhibitor with a part time attending veterinarian under formal arrangements shall establish and maintain a written 
program of veterinary care. 
 
Correction date: March 24, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
